posts.raw_content:
Everyone keeps saying Trump doesn’t know what he’s doing. That America is stumbling around the world stage, alienating allies, starting fires it can’t control. His post in the early hours of this morning will generate the usual outrage. The usual hot takes. The usual missing of the point.


There IS a plan. It is working. And it’s WAY bigger than Iran.


What’s unfolding right now isn’t foreign policy chaos. It’s the deliberate dismantling of the post-war global order and the construction of something new in its place.


Pete Hegseth named it himself last week, standing in front of Latin American military leaders in Florida: “Trump has drawn a new strategic map, from Greenland to the Gulf of America to the Panama Canal and its surrounding countries. At the Department of War, we call this strategic map the Greater North America.”


This is a full reset. And every piece is moving exactly where Washington wants it.


Start with the Middle East. Iran is now strangling the Strait of Hormuz, with tolls, threats, and instability. Middle Eastern oil becomes unreliable.


The Gulf states are rattled, newly aggressive, and openly on edge after Iranian attacks on their interests. Tehran is weakened enough that it can’t reach nuclear capability, but not so destroyed that the chaos ends.


That chaos is the point. Prolonged instability in the Strait is catastrophic for Gulf oil exporters. It’s a gift for American ones.


Move to Europe. An energy crisis forces the continent off Russian supply, already severed by the Ukraine war, which conveniently keeps Moscow bogged down and broke. Who’s left to fill the gap?


America, which now controls one of the largest oil producers on the planet in Venezuela. Europe will buy US energy, and to get those deals, it will have to buy US debt.


The deficit hawks worried about the debt ceiling should look at who’s being lined up to finance it (and who already holds most of US debt).


Then there’s China. Forty-five per cent of its oil transits the Strait. Venezuela, previously China’s largest oil supplier, is now firmly under American control.


Trump didn't mince his words about controlling Venezuelan oil either, that's deliberate signalling to China, "look what I've got".


Beijing’s leverage in any trade negotiation has just been substantially reduced. The tariff war looks very different when your opponent controls your energy supply.


And NATO? The allies were briefed on Iran. They walked in, failed to show up, and handed Trump exactly the argument he needed on Greenland. That wasn’t an accident either.


This is not a president flailing. This is a strategy being executed across five theatres simultaneously, with oil as the central instrument of power.


If you look at all of that and still think America is losing, just wait until you see what they look like when they’re winning.


Source:  https://web.archive.org/web/20260409151257/https://www.gbnews.com/opinion/donald-trump-iran-war-ceasefire
posts.author: RealityIsAPsyOp
posts.title: Opinion:  America just triumphed on the world stage - and its haters are too blind to see how - Alex Armstrong

At the Department of War, we call this strategic map the Greater North America.”

This is a full reset. And every piece is moving exactly where Washington wants it.

posts.raw_content:
Our president has been saying some pretty wild things lately.

But there's a reason for that. Not only are he and his family personally threatened 24/7,  I think the nation itself and all of us with it are threatened, too, if he doesn't play along at least some of the time.

What could they do to us if he refuses? A short list:

starting more big fires in suburban areas (very easy to do, extremely destructive and very hard to put out)

taking down parts of the power grid, just here and there

something toxic in a city water supply

pulling the plug on some airport radar

sabotaging airplanes or bridges or dams

even a suitcase nuke or dirty bomb is not out of the question at all

There's really no limit to what they could try. The fires seem to be their destruction of choice, with as terrifying as those fires are and the amount of devastation they cause. And it costs nothing to set a few of them simultaneously up on the mountains outside a city.

Trump lives with this every minute of every day. Occasionally he might have to cave to shut them up and save the rest of us. I remember when he talked up the COVID shots some years back and everyone sneered at him for changing his mind/backing off/TACOing - 

except for those of us who thought about it for two minutes and realized he would never say such garbage unless he was trying to protect himself and his family and all of our families, too.

The monsters who make these threats just delight in it, because it's one of the few forms of control over him that they have. They are really really really hoping it will turn his base against him and make him look stupid and crazy in front of the world. 

*Oooh, then maybe then they can 25th him and make his allies turn against him too!!!*

He is neither stupid nor crazy and we know this.

There are other ways besides taking up arms to defend your people. Sometimes it means having to look a little crazy in front of the world, when someone makes an offer you can't refuse -- but who gives a fuck if it saves Phoenix, Arizona or Johnsonville, Tennessee from being "accidentally" torched.

Obvious ploy is obvious. Don't fall for it.  Please.
posts.author: Cuetardian
posts.title: Obvious ploy is obvious. Don't fall for it.  Trump is neither foolish nor crazy, but the monsters trying to wreck our nation would sure like you to think he is. So would the bots and other vermin infesting this site.
